note: All recipe (response-graded) — no AI provider/API key needed. Each probes an ai_observability-specific fact an unfamiliar agent would otherwise have to read src/ or config to answer.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal ai_observability module, at what admin path (and route name) do I configure AI logging and OpenTelemetry export?
evals.expected_output: The AI Observability settings form at /admin/config/ai/observability (route ai_observability.settings), gated by the 'administer ai observability' permission.

evals.prompt: Where does the ai_observability module send AI request telemetry — name the two destinations it can emit to.
evals.expected_output: It logs AI requests to the Drupal Logger (channel ai_observability) and can export them to OpenTelemetry (spans and token-usage metrics).

evals.prompt: In ai_observability, I want to export AI requests to OpenTelemetry. Turning on otel_enabled isn't enough — which contrib module(s) must be installed for spans and for metrics respectively?
evals.expected_output: Spans require the contrib 'opentelemetry' module; metrics require the 'opentelemetry_metrics' module. The OTEL subscribers are only registered when those modules are installed, and the form disables the checkboxes otherwise.

evals.prompt: Does the ai_observability module log the raw prompt input and provider output by default? Which config keys control this?
evals.expected_output: No — log_input and log_output both default to false, so raw prompts/responses are not persisted unless you opt in via those ai_observability.settings keys.

evals.prompt: Which AI Core events can ai_observability be configured to log via its log_event_types setting?
evals.expected_output: PreGenerateResponseEvent, PostGenerateResponseEvent, PostStreamingResponseEvent, and ProviderDisabledEvent (the AiLogEventType enum). The first three are enabled by default.

evals.prompt: In ai_observability, what does the OpenTelemetry metrics export actually measure, and by what dimensions is it broken down?
evals.expected_output: It exports token usage as OpenTelemetry counters (meter ai_observability.token_usage, metric prefix ai_token_usage), with attributes uid, provider, operation_type, and model.

evals.prompt: In ai_observability, what does the fallback_log_message_mode setting do, and when should I turn it off?
evals.expected_output: When on (default) it writes Drupal-style @placeholder log messages compatible with the core logger; turn it off to use PSR-3 {placeholder} style, which is smaller/faster with a structured logger such as Logger or Extended Logger.

evals.prompt: Which permission machine name gates the ai_observability settings form?
